steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage traces water from Alaska's Susitna Glacier through a chain of rivers. The sentence after the blank describes the endpoint of that journey: the Susitna's mouth, where the water empties into the Gulf of Alaska. The blank must logically introduce this final destination.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'There,': "There" is a locational adverb meaning "at that place." It points to the Susitna's mouth just mentioned in the preceding sentence, signaling arrival at the journey's endpoint. ✓
  • Choice B — 'For instance,': "For instance" introduces an example. The sentence isn't giving an example of something; it's describing the final destination. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Regardless,': "Regardless" signals that something happens despite an obstacle or contrary condition. No contrast is being made here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Instead,': "Instead" signals an alternative to what was just described. The water emptying into the Gulf isn't an alternative to traveling the rivers — it's the continuation and conclusion of that journey. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
